text: This is the VOA Special English Technology
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语科技节目。

text: Report. Call it medicine on a microchip.
zh: [1] 报道栏目。可以称之为“微芯片上的药物”。

text: Researchers in the United States have developed the first wirelessly controlled device that
zh: [2] 美国研究人员研发出了首个无线控制装置，

text: can supply a drug directly into the body.
zh: [3] 能够直接向体内输送药物。

text: A small chip is implanted under the skin.
zh: [4] 一枚小芯片被植入皮肤下。

text: It contains the medicine, which it releases at preset times.
zh: [5] 芯片中储存着药物，会在预设时间释放。

text: The developers say the device could improve the lives of millions of people
zh: [6] 研发者称，这种装置有望改善数百万人的生活，

text: who take medicine for long -term illnesses.
zh: [7] 尤其是那些长期患病、需要长期服药的人。

text: A company called Microchips began developing the device about 15 years ago.
zh: [8] 一家名为Microchips的公司大约15年前开始研发这种装置。

text: In February, the company released the results of its first successful tests in humans.
zh: [9] 今年2月，该公司公布了首次人体试验成功的结果。

text: The tests took place in Denmark with seven women with osteoporosis.
zh: [10] 试验在丹麦进行，参与者是七名患有骨质疏松症的女性。

text: causes bones to become weak and break easily.
zh: [11] 这种病会使骨骼变得脆弱，容易骨折。

text: The disorder is common among older people, especially women.
zh: [12] 这种病在老年人中很常见，尤其是女性。

text: Many patients have to give themselves daily injections of medicine.
zh: [13] 许多患者不得不每天给自己注射药物。

text: One type of treatment requires injections for two years.
zh: [14] 有一种治疗方法需要连续注射两年。

text: Robert Farah is the president of Microchips.
zh: [15] 罗伯特·法拉是Microchips公司的总裁。

text: He says many patients stop taking the medicine because of the pain
zh: [16] 他说，许多患者因为注射带来的疼痛和压力而停止用药。

text: and stress of the injections. As a result,
only 25 % of the patients will go through the entire 24 months of treatment.
zh: [18] 只有25%的患者能坚持完成整整24个月的疗程。

text: The microchip is a few centimeters long.
zh: [19] 这枚微芯片只有几厘米长。

text: It has small sections that each hold a single dose of medicine.
zh: [20] 它内部有许多小格，每个格子装有一剂药物。

text: Mr. Farah says the device has to be programmed with the times to release the drug.
zh: [21] 法拉说，这种装置需要预先设定释放药物的时间。

text: For osteoporosis, the physician will program the device,
zh: [22] 对于骨质疏松症患者，医生会为装置设定程序，

text: and the device has the ability to release a dose at a given time, every single day.
zh: [23] 使其能够在每天的固定时间释放一剂药物。

text: For other diseases where the physician may want to alter the dosing schedule,
zh: [24] 而对于其他疾病，如果医生需要调整给药时间表，

text: they will have the ability to wirelessly reprogram that dosing schedule.
zh: [25] 他们可以通过无线方式重新设定给药计划。

text: He says doctors will be able to reprogram the device from a computer or even a cell phone.
zh: [26] 他说，医生将能通过电脑甚至手机远程重新编程该装置。

text: The seven women in the study were ages 65 to 70.
zh: [27] 参与研究的七名女性年龄在65到70岁之间。

text: The researchers say the implants were just as effective as daily injections.
zh: [28] 研究人员称，这种植入装置的效果与每日注射一样好。

text: And they say the dosage amounts were more exact than patients often give themselves.
zh: [29] 而且他们表示，其给药剂量比患者自行注射时更为精确。

text: The microchips in the study held only 20 doses of medicine.
zh: [30] 研究中使用的微芯片只能容纳20剂药物。

text: Mr. Farah says it could take four years to design and test a version
zh: [31] 法拉说，要设计并测试出能容纳一年用量的版本，

text: with the ability to hold a year's worth of medicine.
zh: [32] 可能还需要四年时间。

text: He says the microchip may one day free patients from having to remember to take their medicine
zh: [33] 他说，这种微芯片或许有朝一日能让患者不必再记得按时服药，

text: or give themselves injections.
zh: [34] 也不用再自己注射。

text: It may also be useful in treating other chronic diseases,
zh: [35] 它还可能有助于治疗其他慢性疾病，

text: including heart disease and multiple sclerosis.
zh: [36] 包括心脏病和多发性硬化症。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Cresutti.
zh: [37] 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是卡罗琳·克雷苏蒂。
